skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Diagnoses long-context failure patterns such as lost-in-middle recall, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and contradictory context clash.
skill.summary: Identify and mitigate context degradation patterns in long-running AI agent sessions.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious behavior, prompt injection, network access, subprocess execution, or credential handling. The high weak-cryptography and medium external-command static findings are false positives caused by topic words and markdown/code examples; the remaining risk is a benign local Python analysis script in a community skill.

low_findings.title: Local Analysis Script Included
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Python utility that analyzes provided context text with numpy, regex matching, and stdout printing. I found no evidence of filesystem writes, network calls, subprocess execution, environment access, or credential handling, but packaged scripts in community skills still deserve review before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full script uses local computation and print output only. Confidence is high because targeted search found no subprocess, network, filesystem write, or environment access patterns.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Findings
low_findings.description: The static external-command hits point to markdown fences and example blocks, not executable shell backticks or runtime command invocation. No evidence found of subprocess, system, exec, or eval usage in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation boundaries or examples, and targeted search found no runtime command execution APIs. This strongly supports dismissal as false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Findings
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits are caused by semantic terms such as degradation, poisoning, and alert thresholds in context-engineering documentation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content discusses context reliability rather than cryptography. Targeted search found no cryptographic API usage, so the static matches are best explained as keyword collisions.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Findings
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance hits are natural-language phrases inside context-clash and hallucination-marker examples. No evidence found of host enumeration, OS probing, process listing, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain conceptual documentation and string markers, not system inspection code. The broader script review found no reconnaissance APIs.

content.value_statement: Long AI conversations can lose important details, repeat mistakes, or mix conflicting instructions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured way to identify context failure patterns and choose mitigations.
actual_capabilities: Explains lost-in-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and context clash patterns.
Helps diagnose agent failures caused by long or noisy conversation history.
Recommends mitigations such as compaction, task isolation, filtering, and context placement.
Provides a local Python utility for estimating context health and degradation indicators.
Offers reference patterns for monitoring attention placement, relevance, and poisoning signals.
limitations: The Python detector uses heuristic simulation, not actual model attention weights.
Model benchmark details may become outdated as providers release new systems.
It does not automatically repair prompts, memory stores, or retrieval pipelines.
It requires human judgment to confirm root causes in production incidents.

use_cases.title: Debug Long Agent Sessions
use_cases.description: Identify whether degraded output quality comes from middle-context neglect, stale assumptions, or accumulated errors.

use_cases.title: Design Reliable Context Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan context placement, compaction, and filtering strategies before deploying long-context workflows.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Retrieval Quality
use_cases.description: Review retrieved documents for distraction, contradiction, and poisoning risks before they enter a model context.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Conversation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner diagnosis of a single failing session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this conversation for context degradation. Identify the most likely pattern and suggest three concrete fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Lost-in-Middle Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this prompt or context layout for lost-in-middle risk. Mark which details should move to the beginning or end.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Retrieval Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of RAG or tool-provided context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these retrieved documents for distraction, poisoning, and clash. Recommend what to include, summarize, or exclude.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Mitigation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced system design for production workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context degradation mitigation plan for this agent architecture. Include monitoring signals, thresholds, and recovery steps.

output_examples.input: A support agent starts ignoring billing constraints after a long troubleshooting thread.
output_examples.output: Likely pattern: lost-in-middle with possible context distraction. Move billing constraints to the active task header, summarize older troubleshooting details, and keep recent user intent near the end.

output_examples.input: A coding agent keeps using a deprecated API after the user corrected it twice.
output_examples.output: Likely pattern: context poisoning. Remove or explicitly invalidate the stale API reference, restate the verified current API, and restart from a clean task summary if errors persist.

output_examples.input: A RAG workflow retrieves five related documents, but two describe older product behavior.
output_examples.output: Likely pattern: context clash. Apply version filtering, label the current source of truth, and exclude outdated documents from the active answer context.

best_practices: Place critical goals, constraints, and verified facts near the start or end of active context.
Filter retrieved content for relevance and version before adding it to the model window.
Trigger compaction or task isolation before context length reaches known degradation thresholds.
anti_patterns: Do not keep every tool output in active context when a compact reference is enough.
Do not mix unrelated task histories in one session without clear boundaries.
Do not assume a larger context window always improves answer quality.

faq.question: What is context degradation?
faq.answer: It is reduced model performance caused by long, noisy, stale, or conflicting context.

faq.question: Does this skill run external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network calls or external service use in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Can it measure real attention weights?
faq.answer: No. The included script simulates attention patterns and recommends using model-specific tools for production measurement.

faq.question: When should I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Use it when a coding session becomes inconsistent, forgets constraints, or repeats corrected mistakes.

faq.question: Can it fix a degraded conversation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It recommends recovery strategies, but a user or agent must apply them.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit rates it low risk because static high-risk findings were false positives and no malicious intent was found.
